arguments.task: Research Jira using official Atlassian and first-party sources only. Open official Jira Cloud Platform REST API docs, Jira Software Cloud REST API docs, Atlassian OAuth 2.0 docs, Atlassian basic auth/API token docs for Jira Cloud, Atlassian pricing or access pages relevant to developer access, Atlassian rate limiting docs for Jira Cloud, and official Atlassian MCP server or official public GitHub/MCP registry evidence if available. Extract concise evidence for: one-line product description, auth methods, whether API access is self-serve or gated/paid, API surface type and breadth, MCP existence, buildability today, rate limits, and notable signals. Return the exact supporting URLs visited and short supporting quotes or facts.

input.thought: Launch a focused browser research run on official Jira sources.

raw_result.description: Jira Cloud provides REST APIs for project, issue, workflow, and software development management in Atlassian workspaces.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official Jira Cloud docs show OAuth 2.0 (3LO) for apps, basic auth using email plus API token for REST APIs, and additional Atlassian app frameworks/auth patterns such as Forge/Connect; exact toolkit auth choice depends on deployment model.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official developer documentation for Jira Cloud REST APIs is publicly available and self-serve; developers can create Atlassian accounts and API tokens without partner approval.

api_surface.breadth: Broad public Jira Cloud REST API coverage across Jira Platform v3 and Jira Software Cloud endpoints for issues, projects, workflows, users, boards, sprints, and related resources.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Atlassian documentation navigation exposed an official 'Atlassian MCP server' entry and a support URL for Atlassian Rovo MCP Server, suggesting an official MCP offering, but Jira-specific scope was not fully verified from the gathered evidence.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official Jira Cloud rate-limiting docs exist, including general Jira Cloud platform rate limiting and Data Center/App migration-related rate limiting pages; exact limits appear endpoint/context-dependent rather than a single universal quota.
raw_result.notable_signals: Atlassian maintains separate Jira Platform and Jira Software REST API documentation, dedicated basic-auth guidance, and dedicated rate-limiting documentation. Official docs navigation also references an Atlassian MCP server, a positive signal for agent/tooling support.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Evidence gathered supports Jira Cloud REST APIs, auth options, and rate-limit docs, but the pricing/access tier beyond basic self-serve account creation and the precise official MCP scope for Jira were not fully validated from page contents. The MCP URL also had loading issues during browsing, so MCP details should be confirmed by a human.
